name: Bill Lee
abstract: William James Edwards Lee III (July 23, 1928 – May 24, 2023) was an American jazz bassist and composer, known for his collaborations with Bob Dylan and Aretha Franklin, his compositions for jazz percussionist Max Roach, and his session work as a "first-call" musician and band leader to many of the twentieth-century's most significant mus…

interviews.title: Last Straws: John Chaffe, Bris Jones, Bill Lee, Frank De La Houssaye, Bob Ice Interview, 1960-04-18

works_created.t: Mo' Better Blues: Mo' Better Blues
Zachary and the Scaly Bark Tree

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: those of us who weren't there then - through records, seem to indicate to us that the people who were really doin' it... were Sidney Bechet and Louis Armstrong... yourself, Driver, Bill Lee... solos of Driver, Lee and yourself in the Charlie Miller Jazz Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Lee had written this music and it was beautiful because he knew how to write for basses... We never got a chance to play… we played down at the Vanguard one night... Bill Lee, he had a pair pliers... But he knew what he was doing and we really loved him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: I had the opportunity to participate in film scores that other people had written, like Terence Blanchard or Spike Lee – Bill Lee, Spike Lee’s father. I did several of Spike Lee’s first films, the soundtracks.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rufus Reid
about_quotes.evidence: I got a chance to go to Bill Lee’s house over in Brooklyn at ten o’clock in the morning on a Saturday, and Milt was there... Richard Davis and Bill Lee and Lyle Atkinson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rufus Reid interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, GA, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Max Fleming
about_quotes.evidence: And one night I was there and I guess Bill Lee would have sent me to the Top of the Gate, I was playing with him. I came down and there’s Bill Lee standing there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Max Fleming interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, June 8, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: He was on one of my favorite record dates “Rakin’ and Scrapin’.” Hugh Walker is on that. Hugh Walker, George Coleman, Blue Mitchell and Bill Lee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010
